findingDetails.title: Marker on-disk shape drifts from spec-mandated jobId field
findingDetails.description: The writer serializes `{ childPid, activeUntilMs, labels, refreshedAtIso }`, but spec.md requires `{ childPid, activeUntilMs, jobId, refreshedAtIso }`. The reader currently ignores the extra/missing field, so the runtime path is intact, but the contract is drifted and tests/stress harness still reference `jobId`.

findingDetails.title: Spec docs reference stale mcp_server/bin paths
findingDetails.description: The Files to Change table lists `mcp_server/bin/lib/model-server-supervision.cjs` and `mcp_server/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs`, but the shipped implementation is under `.opencode/bin/`. This path drift makes the spec and tasks stale for future maintainers.

findingDetails.title: Duplicate labels possible in maintenance marker
findingDetails.description: `activeLabels` is a plain array without deduplication. Overlapping holders with the same label (e.g., two concurrent `index_scan` jobs) leave duplicate entries until a refresh rewrites the file, producing a misleading on-disk label set.

findingDetails.title: Marker write failures are silently dropped
findingDetails.description: `writeMarker` discards the boolean result of `atomicWriteFile`. If the disk write fails (full disk, permission error, etc.), the daemon believes it is protected but the marker file is absent or stale, leaving it exposed to reaping with no logged error.

findingDetails.title: Implementation summary is stale about embedding-queue coverage
findingDetails.description: `implementation-summary.md` states the post-scan background-embedding queue is unprotected and is follow-on work, but `retry-manager.ts` already wraps embedding-queue bursts with `beginMaintenance('embedding-queue')`. The doc is now stale and could misroute a future fix.

results.summary: Normative marker-shape claim is partially met: runtime behavior matches, but the serialized field name (`labels`) diverges from the spec (`jobId`).
Implementation summary records build, syntax, unit, and harness checks as passing; no unsupported completion marks observed in this pass.

noveltyJustification: All five findings are new to this lineage. F001 is the highest-impact drift because it changes the on-disk contract the launcher and tests both consume.

downgradeTrigger: If the spec is updated to require `labels` instead of `jobId`, or if the reader is changed to validate `jobId` and tolerates its absence, downgrade to P2.

transitions.reason: Initial discovery: spec field name diverges from shipped implementation
